{{Infobox book
| name = The Knight
| slug = lh-03-the-knight
| precedes = [[The Pilot (novel)|The Pilot]]
| follows = [[The Engineer (novel)|The Engineer]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-lh-03-the-knight.jpg|250px]]
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The story follows [[Varic Vallenar]], a young mage who transitions from a defeated student to a powerful [[Titan Knight]]. After losing an arena duel to his mentor, [[Kyri Prest]], Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic is thrust into the war zone of the [[Lesser Karoshan system]]. His journey takes a pivotal turn when he accidentally bonds with the [[Blue Divine Titan]], officially becoming a [[Titan Knight]] alongside his teammate [[Raion Raithe]]. As Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic trains under Kyri Prest{{!}}Kyri, he learns to balance his unique water magic with the immense natural talent of [[Raion]].

As the crew of the starship *[[The Last Horizon]]* navigates a galaxy fraught with peril, they face multiple existential threats. The [[D’Niss]]—a swarm of psychic insectoids—return to threaten the galaxy, eventually manifesting their terrifying Calamities. The crew also contends with political instability within the [[Karoshan fleet]], the influence of the '[[Perfected]]' faction led by the usurper [[Felrex]], and the manipulative Shadow{{!}}shadow organization [[Solstice]]. Amidst these conflicts, the crew seeks to secure the [[Zenith Cannon]], a sentient plasma weapon known as [[Mortal Edict]], which ultimately chooses the crew member [[Omega]] as its wielder.

A central motivation for the crew is saving [[Parryl Cryce]], a former [[Green Knight]] suffering from incurable [[D’Niss]] parasite{{!}}parasites. To save her, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic and [[Raion]] embark on a dangerous mission to the planet [[Visiria]] to retrieve the The Heart of Visiria{{!}}Heart of Visiria. This mission leads to intense diplomatic friction and a brutal series of arena duels on [[Visiria]], where [[Raion]] is nearly destroyed by a political trap. Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic eventually intervenes, systematically defeating Visirian champions to defend his friend's honor. After successfully retrieving the Heart, the crew uses its power to resurrect [[Parryl]].

The conflict reaches a climax with the arrival of [[Esh’kinaar]], the star-eating [[Swarm-Queen]]. As the [[D’Niss]] attempt a massive ritual to summon her, the crew of *[[The Last Horizon]]* engages in a desperate space battle. [[Raion]] pilots the [[Red Divine Titan]] into a suicidal charge to protect the galaxy, while Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic takes command of the [[Blue Divine Titan]]. In a coordinated final assault, the [[Knights]] manage to defeat [[Esh’kinaar]] and her subsequent black-magic incarnation. Following the victory, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic decides to shift from a reactive stance to an offensive one, planning to confront the remaining threats in the galaxy, even as [[Benri Vallenar]] is recruited by [[Solstice]].}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|After losing a televised arena duel to his mentor, [[Kyri Prest]], Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic feels defeated by his inability to match the physical prowess of the [[Visiri]]. Kyri Prest{{!}}Kyri ignores his self-pity and takes him to a war zone in the [[Lesser Karoshan system]] to face the [[Iron Legion]]. Despite his injuries and fear, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic is forced into immediate combat alongside the [[Sixty-Fifth Mage Corps]] as they defend a [[Karoshan]] fortification.}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Five years ago, [[Raion]] and the four other [[Titan Knights]] prepare for a high-stakes mission to kill one of the [[Seven Calamities]]. After a moment of team bonding, [[Raion]] receives an urgent video message from [[Doctor Cryce]] announcing that the [[D’Niss]] are retreating, meaning victory is within reach if they defeat the remaining threat, [[Neth’terith]]. Encouraged by this news and new intelligence from the [[Galactic Union]], [[Raion]] rushes to share the update with his teammates.}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|While attempting to reinforce the seals on an ancient lich within a moon-sized tomb, the Captain struggles against the undead mage's power and the distractions of a crewmate named [[Omega]]. After the lich breaks free, [[Omega]] intervenes with heavy weaponry to help the Captain destroy the skeletal sorcerer and claim his golden staff. Upon returning to the ship, the [[Horizon]] informs the Captain that while the rest of the crew is not in immediate danger, they are in significant trouble.}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|After materializing at a [[Galactic Union]] outpost, the narrator joins forces with [[Sola]] to combat an infestation of [[Scourge Warrens]], eventually clearing fifty of the fifty-six locations. The narrator then teleports to the city of [[Old Tolyer]] to assist [[Mell]], who is being besieged by bounty hunters seeking information on a [[Zenith Cannon]] device. Following a brief skirmish where the narrator uses magic to subdue the attackers, [[Omega]] arrives with the coordinates [[Mell]] requires, allowing them to prepare for departure.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The Captain visits [[Queen Shyrax]] on her starship to discuss the growing influence of [[the Perfected]] and the usurper [[Felrex]] within her fleet. After advising her on how to handle the sabotage and fanaticism caused by [[Gharyas]], the Captain agrees to cast a pathfinding spell to identify traitors. The spell reveals widespread betrayal throughout the [[Karoshan fleet]], prompting Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax to begin personal inspections while the Captain prepares to return to [[The Last Horizon]].}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Raion Raithe]], a [[Knight of The Last Horizon]], rescues a woman and her daughter from predatory, acid-spitting aliens on the industrial colony [[Pekler-76]]. As [[Raion]] struggles to fight off the overwhelming swarm while protecting the humans, his companion Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic arrives via magical summons to assist. With Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic providing magical protection and guidance, [[Raion]] is able to fight more effectively to search for other survivors.}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|After defeating the final [[D’Niss]] calamity, [[Raion]] realizes that his fellow [[Knights]] have been replaced by parasite{{!}}parasites following an encounter with a mysterious cloud of insects. Despite his hesitation to kill his friends, [[Raion]] is forced into a violent confrontation on the carrier as the impostors attack him. Ultimately, [[Raion]] destroys the possessed [[Knights]] to stop the parasite{{!}}parasites, though he vows to try and save [[Parryl]].}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The crew gathers for a meeting where the ship's spirit, [[Horizon]], reveals that the [[Zenith Cannon]] has been traced to a [[Galactic Union]] inspector at a scrapper colony. Despite the Captain's concerns regarding his father, who is the [[Union]] representative, the crew votes to pursue and secure the weapon. The meeting concludes abruptly when [[Raion]] receives a mysterious call claiming his friend is alive.}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|After investigating damaged [[Iron Hives]] that appear to have been struck by a single massive force, Kyri Prest{{!}}Kyri and Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic travel to the [[Titan Force]] to meet [[Doctor Janbell Cryce]]. While Kyri Prest{{!}}Kyri fails to establish a connection with the advanced [[Blue Divine Titan]], Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic accidentally activates the mech simply by touching its armor. [[Doctor Cryce]] confirms that Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic has successfully bonded with the machine, officially welcoming him as a new [[Titan Knight]].}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Varic Vallenar]] and [[Raion]] arrive at the abandoned and dilapidated [[Titan Force headquarters]] on [[Delsin]] to meet [[Doctor Cryce]]. After navigating a warehouse containing the [[Divine Titans]], they encounter an aged and weary [[Cryce]], who reveals she has been struggling with unsuccessful biological research. The chapter concludes with [[Cryce]] offering to show them her niece, whom she has been unsuccessfully attempting to save.}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Parryl Cryce]], a former [[Green Knight]], lies in stasis suffering from incurable parasite{{!}}parasites that are slowly killing her. The Captain proposes a magical ritual to save her, but explains it requires the The Heart of Visiria{{!}}Heart of Visiria, a powerful restorative artifact located on the dangerous planet [[Visiria]]. After the Captain uses magic to slow [[Parryl]]'s physical decline, [[Doctor Cryce]] reluctantly agrees to let [[Raion]] attempt to retrieve the artifact.}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|On his first day as the [[Blue Titan Knight]], [[Varic Vallenar]] meets his new teammates—[[Aelora]], [[Javik]], [[Parryl]], and [[Raion]]—and participates in combat training led by his instructor, Kyri Prest{{!}}Kyri. During practice duels, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic gains confidence by successfully using water magic against his peers, but he is humbled after witnessing Kyri Prest{{!}}Kyri's overwhelming power during a match with [[Raion]]. Following the training, Kyri Prest{{!}}Kyri warns Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic not to compare himself to [[Raion]]'s immense natural talent and advises him to focus on his unique role as a wizard.}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic and [[Raion]] arrive at the planet [[Visiria]] via a borrowed shuttle to begin a mission involving the The Heart of Visiria{{!}}Heart of Visiria. During the flight, [[Raion]] reflects on his strained relationship with his family and his doubts about his role as a Knight. Upon landing, they meet [[Ryzer Raithe]], who expresses concern over their lack of disguises, but Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic demonstrates the need for caution by using magic to expose and neutralize a [[Karoshan]] spy embedded in the crowd.}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|At [[Ryzer Raithe]]'s villa, the narrator and [[Raion]] prepare for a diplomatic mission where [[Raion]] must meet with Visirian [[Senators]] to secure access to the planet's treasure and save a friend. After confirming that the The Heart of Visiria{{!}}Heart of Visiria is on-planet and no immediate traps exist, the narrator returns to the ship only to find that three planets have mysteriously lost contact with their [[Subline]]. The crew decides to split up, sending the narrator and [[Sola]] to investigate the silent planets while [[Raion]] continues his diplomatic efforts on [[Visiria]].}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The protagonist engages in a tense negotiation with her father, [[Benri Vallenar]], proposing that they collaborate to study a [[Zenith Device]] he recently acquired. Following the call, [[Sola]] suggests stealing the device rather than negotiating, leading to a philosophical debate about morality and conflict. The chapter concludes with the two characters sharing intimate reflections on their past lives and experiences with death while traveling through subspace.}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|While navigating a [[Subspace]] blockade, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic uses [[pathfinding magic]] to discover that the [[D’Niss]], a swarm of psychic insectoids, are responsible for the obstruction. The crew is ambushed by [[Sel’miroth]], one of the [[Seven Calamities]], whose psychic influence nearly overwhelms Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic before [[Sola]]'s [[Worldslayer]] weapon intervenes. Despite a successful escape into [[Subspace]], Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic is left shaken by the realization that the [[D’Niss]] have returned.}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic informs [[Raion]] that the [[D’Niss]] are returning and instructs him to pursue diplomatic ties on [[Visiria]] to secure future aid. Meanwhile, the crew of [[The Last Horizon]] discusses their limited resources for ship production and debates how to provide [[Shadow Ark]] with a planet. The chapter concludes when an urgent distress call from the Royal Guard of Karosha’s Dragon Court{{!}}Karoshan royal guard prompts Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic to order an immediate intervention.}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|After emerging from [[Subspace]] to find Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax's fleet embroiled in a civil war caused by [[Perfected]] agents, the crew of [[The Last Horizon]] organizes a boarding mission to reclaim hijacked ships. [[Sola]] and [[Omega]] successfully clear the bridge of one hostile vessel, while [[Mell]] uses a decoy-bot to assist Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax in liberating loyal prisoners and gaining access to enemy data. The chapter concludes with the team working together to secure information from the captured starship before it can be deleted.}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|After [[Mell]] discovers evidence of the [[Galactic Union]] supporting King Regent{{!}}King Regent Felrex, the crew discusses the possibility that the Shadow{{!}}shadow organization [[Solstice]] is manipulating various galactic powers against them. The group debates their next moves, ultimately deciding to prioritize retrieving the [[Zenith Cannon]] to balance their power against rising threats like the [[D’Niss]]. [[Omega]] suggests that instead of playing defense, they should use the overwhelming might of their The Zenith Starship{{!}}Zenith Starship to confront their enemies directly.}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Titan Knights]] engage in a high-stakes battle against Neth’therith, a [[D’Niss]] Calamity that is attacking a planet and its [[World Spirit]]. Despite [[Raion]]'s reckless decision to summon his [[Divine Titan]] early, the team coordinates their unique elemental and technological powers to launch a massive, synchronized assault. The combined strike successfully destroys the giant insect, though [[Raion]] is left drifting in space after exhausting his [[Titan]]'s power.}}
* '''Chapter 21:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Benri Vallenar]] meets his son Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic on the neutral planet [[Beshkin]], arriving with a team of experts and extensive surveillance decoys to negotiate for the [[Zenith Cannon]]. Upon boarding the starship [[The Last Horizon]], [[Benri]] attempts to manipulate the meeting through political posturing with [[Queen Shyrax]] and by using magical decoys to hide [[the Cannon]]. However, the ship's [[World Spirit]] easily bypasses [[Benri]]'s primitive concealment spells, delivering the [[Zenith Cannon]] directly to Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic.}}
* '''Chapter 22:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Varic Vallenar]] discovers that the [[Zenith Cannon]] is a sentient, highly advanced plasma pistol named [[Mortal Edict]]. After [[Sola]] briefly passes the weapon's trials, [[the Cannon]] ultimately chooses [[Omega]], a reckless individual, as its wielder. Despite the chaos caused by the weapon's immense power and personality, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic concludes that the [[Zenith Cannon]] has officially joined their crew.}}
* '''Chapter 23:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|After the protagonist's father departs under suspicious circumstances, Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax decides to become the guardian of [[Shadow Ark]] to provide a secure staging ground for her people. [[Archmage Vallenar]] suggests a young [[World Spirit]] planet as a potential location for this new base, which the protagonist agrees to visit. The chapter concludes with the arrival of [[Omega]] and [[Lemon]], who reveals she is busy rebuilding the Advocates and dealing with a cult attempting to free a demon king.}}
* '''Chapter 24:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|On the planet [[Basyrryx]], which features a vast habitable cavern system, the protagonist conducts an extensive magic class for a diverse group of students including [[Karoshan]] royals, professional mages, a [[World Spirit]], and a ghost. The protagonist demonstrates various disciplines such as [[binding]], [[elementalism]], summoning, and pathfinding, while explaining the complexities of their unique magical abilities. Despite the educational setting, the protagonist feels a sense of unease regarding the ongoing D’Niss{{!}}D'Niss advance and the safety of their crew members elsewhere in the galaxy.}}
* '''Chapter 25:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Raion Raithe]], a member of the [[Titan Force]], navigates his diplomatic duties on the planet [[Visiria]] alongside his brother, Ryzer Raithe{{!}}Ryzer. While approaching a meeting with the [[Planetary Governor]] at the [[Prime Arena]], [[Raion]]'s presence causes the arena's champion statues to awaken and bow to him. Despite Ryzer Raithe{{!}}Ryzer's visible anxiety and warnings to avoid conflict, a confident [[Raion]] pushes forward into the meeting.}}
* '''Chapter 26:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Raion Raithe]] meets with [[Planetary Governor]] [[Nelula Seiren]] and a group of influential [[Visiri]] senators who express distrust toward him and his crew due to their recent political conflicts. To restore his honor and prevent the [[Senate]] from declaring [[The Last Horizon]] an enemy of [[Visiria]], [[Raion]] agrees to a duel against the [[Prime Arena]]'s reigning champion, [[Albedon Pegarthus]]. While [[Raion]] believes he is fighting for his crew's reputation, Ryzer Raithe{{!}}Ryzer and the Governor secretly prepare for a match that is designed to destroy him.}}
* '''Chapter 27:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Raion Raithe]] enters the [[Prime Arena]] to represent [[The Last Horizon]] in an honor duel against [[Albedon Pegarthus]], eventually winning the fight despite sustaining several injuries. Following his victory, [[Raion]] realizes he has been caught in a trap designed to wear him down through a series of seven consecutive duels against different [[Senators]]' representatives. Despite [[Albedon]]'s warnings to surrender, [[Raion]] accepts a new challenge from [[Senator Krakal]]'s representative to defend his ship's reputation.}}
* '''Chapter 28:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|After a grueling series of arena fights, [[Raion Raithe]] secures a victory by drawing on the faith of his friends to overcome extreme exhaustion and injury. Meanwhile, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Captain Varic wakes from an ominous dream and senses a magical disturbance, leading him to realize [[Raion]] is in danger. Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic mobilizes his crew and orders the ship into [[Subspace]] at maximum speed to reach [[Visiria]] and rescue [[Raion]].}}
* '''Chapter 29:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Following [[Raion]]'s brutal defeat in the [[Prime Arena]], [[Governor Seiren]] attempts to manage the public fallout while Ryzer Raithe{{!}}Ryzer watches in shock. Varic Vallenar{{!}}Captain Varic Vallenar arrives unexpectedly and, after reviewing the footage of the fight, issues a formal challenge to the Visirian leadership. Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic quickly defeats the Arena [[Champion]], [[Albedon]], with a powerful magic attack and issues a new challenge to [[Senator Krakal]].}}
* '''Chapter 30:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Varic Vallenar]] systematically defeats a series of [[Visiri]] fighters and senators in the arena using powerful elemental and Binding magic{{!}}binding magic. After breaking into the [[VIP box]], he confronts [[Governor Seiren]] to demand compensation for the humiliation of his friend, [[Raion]], and threatens the entire planet. The chapter concludes with Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic issuing a death challenge to Ryzer Raithe{{!}}Ryzer before being interrupted by the arrival of a heavily injured [[Raion]].}}
* '''Chapter 31:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The narrator reflects on a past timeline where his teacher, Kyri Prest{{!}}Kyri, retired only to be captured and killed by [[Karoshan forces]]. He also recounts the death of his comrade [[Parryl]] during a mission against D’Niss{{!}}D'Niss soldiers, an event that led to a heated argument with [[Raion]] regarding whether the tragedy could have been prevented. Ultimately, the narrator notes that he was the only original [[Titan Knight]] to survive into the end of that life.}}
* '''Chapter 32:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The narrator explores the specialized medical facilities of [[The Last Horizon]], noting how each crew member's chamber is tailored to their unique biology and needs. After a Nova-Bots{{!}}Nova-Bot confirms that [[Raion]] is stable following his injuries, the narrator visits him and manages his restless attempts to return to duty. During their conversation, [[Raion]] expresses guilt over the failed mission and defends his brother Ryzer Raithe{{!}}Ryzer, eventually falling asleep after being sedated by the medical robot.}}
* '''Chapter 33:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|After returning to the bridge, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Captain Vallenar finds the crew surrounded by a hostile Visirian fleet and an incoming [[Karoshan]] presence, compounded by a massive D’Niss{{!}}D'Niss incursion. Following a tense holographic confrontation with King Regent{{!}}King Regent Felrex, Vallenar successfully negotiates to remain on her ship until the D’Niss{{!}}D'Niss threat is addressed. She then devises a plan to monitor the battle, hoping the Karoshan{{!}}Karoshans will either draw away planetary security or eventually require her crew's assistance.}}
* '''Chapter 34:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The narrator reflects on a past life as a [[Titan Knight]], detailing the team's second generation and their eventual discovery of the massive, star-eating [[Swarm-Queen]], [[Esh’kinaar]]. Upon locating her in [[Dark Space]], the [[Knights]] and a supporting fleet fell into a trap set by three of the [[Seven Calamities]], resulting in a desperate battle to escape. Although they survived the encounter, the narrator and [[Raion]] realize that the [[Swarm-Queen]]'s immense power poses an existential threat to the galaxy, leading them to conclude they must strike first rather than wait for her to attack.}}
* '''Chapter 35:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|As the [[Karoshan fleet]] battles the D’Niss{{!}}D'Niss creature [[Hesh’ellik]], the Infester uses probability magic to cause technical failures and gaps in the fleet's defenses. After a [[Perfected]] dreadnought launches a powerful [[Combat Art]] that destroys the D’Niss{{!}}D'Niss, the crew of [[The Last Horizon]] prepares to teleport onto the enemy ship to strike [[Felrex]]. However, their plan is thwarted when they realize the battle was a trap specifically designed to counter them, as more D’Niss{{!}}D'Niss emerge from subspace.}}
* '''Chapter 36:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|As the crew of [[The Last Horizon]] prepares to fight their way through a [[Visiri]] blockade, Captain [[Felrex]] engages D’Niss{{!}}D'Niss invaders using his [[Perfected]] forces. Meanwhile, the crew member [[Omega]] boards a [[Visiri]] cruiser to cause chaos, leading to a violent confrontation with [[Zyrana]], a [[Perfected]] fighter. The battle ends when [[Zyrana]] uses [[Subspace]] magic to disrupt [[Omega]]'s escape, leaving him wounded and scattered across the ship's floor.}}
* '''Chapter 37:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax engages in a brutal duel with [[Chethriss]], a [[Perfected]] Blade, eventually killing him after the narrator uses Binding magic{{!}}binding magic to assist her. Meanwhile, the crew of [[The Last Horizon]] struggles to maintain defensive shields against a massive [[Karoshan fleet]] and powerful enemy casters. The situation turns dire when [[Omega]] is nearly destroyed and a new, catastrophic threat—one of the [[Seven Calamities]]—emerges in the system, forcing the narrator to order an evacuation.}}
* '''Chapter 38:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|After escaping a [[Visiri]] blockade and securing the The Heart of Visiria{{!}}Heart of Visiria from [[Governor Seiren]], the protagonist leads their battered crew into [[Subspace]] to regroup. Upon arriving at [[Titan Force headquarters]], the protagonist uses the Heart's power to successfully resurrect [[Parryl]]. To ensure their safety while they prepare for battle, the protagonist has [[Shadow Ark]] envelop the moon to protect its inhabitants.}}
* '''Chapter 39:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[The Zenith Starship]] and its assembled fleet enter the [[Visiri system]] to combat a D’Niss{{!}}D'Niss swarm attempting to summon the [[Swarm-Queen]] through a massive ritual. The crew successfully destroys the planet-eating worm Zyn'tharak and two of its magical clones, significantly destabilizing the summoning spell. Meanwhile, [[Alazar]], a powerful mage, observes the battle and orders his corps to proceed with the ritual to release the [[Swarm-Queen]] into the galaxy.}}
* '''Chapter 40:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|As the crew battles to break a D’Niss{{!}}D'Niss summoning spell, the [[World Spirit of Visiria]] kills [[Sel’miroth]], only for the [[Swarm-Queen]] [[Esh’kinaar]] to successfully manifest in their dimension. the Queen{{!}}The Queen's arrival disrupts the protagonist's magic and launches a devastating physical assault that decimates most of the fleet. Realizing they are outmatched, the Captain orders a retreat to seek reinforcements, but panic ensues when [[Raion]] disappears to find [[Ark]].}}
* '''Chapter 41:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Raion]] pilots the [[Red Divine Titan]] into space to engage the D’Niss{{!}}D'Niss, despite Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic's desperate attempts to stop him from a suicidal charge. Following [[Raion]]'s departure, the Captain discovers that the [[Blue Divine Titan]] has unexpectedly responded to them, offering a way to fight alongside [[Raion]]. The Captain accepts the challenge, donning a [[Titan Knight]] uniform to pilot the machine and join the battle.}}
* '''Chapter 42:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|After emerging from [[Subspace]], the Captain uses the [[Blue Titan]] to launch a massive combined elemental and [[Aethertech]] attack against the [[Swarm-Queen]], [[Esh’kinaar]]. Although the strike successfully cracks her shell and damages her limbs, she remains stationary over the sun, prompting the Captain to realize she is likely protecting something or waiting for reinforcements. To counter her strategy of attrition, the Captain coordinates with [[Raion]] and the crew to launch a final, decisive assault.}}
* '''Chapter 43:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Raion]] and the narrator use their [[Divine Titans]] to fight through [[D’Niss soldiers]] before launching a coordinated magical attack that pierces the [[Swarm-Queen]]'s defenses. Following her defeat, the crew debates whether to destroy a mysterious, magically reinforced cocoon left behind by [[Esh’kinaar]]. Ultimately, [[Raion]] and the narrator combine their powers to strike the cocoon, triggering a massive, destructive magical backlash that threatens to destroy them.}}
* '''Chapter 44:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|After surviving a magical cocoon that severely damaged both the Blue and [[Red]] [[Divine Titans]], the crew faces a new, black [[Swarm-Queen]] that possesses the magic of death. As [[Raion]] battles the creature while his mech falls apart, he draws strength from a psychic connection to his many friends across the galaxy. This surge of power allows [[Raion]] to deliver a massive blow, weakening [[the Queen]] enough for Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic to finish it with '[[Absolute Burial]].'}}
* '''Chapter 45:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|After confirming the total destruction of [[Esh’kinaar]] by launching her corpse into a sun, the Captain and [[Horizon]] discuss their remaining enemies on a list of five targets. The Captain decides to stop being reactive and plans to take the fight to Fathom to confront unknown threats. Meanwhile, [[Benri Vallenar]] is contacted by the [[World Spirit]] of the [[Zenith Processor]] and is recruited as an ally for [[Solstice]].}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Raion]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Queen Shyrax]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Horizon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Sola]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Mell]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Swarm-Queen]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Kyri Prest]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Doctor Cryce]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Varic Vallenar]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Visiri]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Esh’kinaar]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Iron King]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Parryl]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Starhammer]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Felrex]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Divine Titans]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Ryzer Raithe]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Sel’miroth]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[King Regent]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Neth’terith]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Green Knight]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Albedon Pegarthus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Hesh’ellik]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Javik]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Nelula Seiren]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Aelora]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Governor Seiren]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Mua Krakal]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Captain (The Last Horizon)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Knight (The Last Horizon)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Benri Vallenar]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Yellow Knight]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Mariala]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Melerius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Raion Raithe]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Alazar]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Gharyas]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Rebel Queen of Karosha]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Senator Krakal]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Zyrana]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Chethriss]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[D’Niss]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Karoshan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Subspace]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Visiria]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Perfected]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Iron Legion]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Galactic Union]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Titan Knights]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Subline]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Shadow Ark]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Dark Space]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Seven Calamities]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Prime Arena]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Alliance (The Last Horizon)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Advocates]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Karosha]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Solstice]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Vallenar Corporation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Titan Force]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Ark]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Karoshan Alliance]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Basyrryx]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Union]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Zenith Colony]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Blue facility]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Titan Knight]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Visiria system]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[pathfinding magic]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Aetheric symbols]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Cosmic Path]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Combat Artist]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Combat Art]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Dance of a Burning World]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Combat Arts]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Mirror of Silence]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|sealing magic}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[The Last Horizon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Zenith Cannon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Divine Titan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Nova-Bots]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Red Divine Titan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Blue Titan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[The Heart of Visiria]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Blue Divine Titan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Zenith Device]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[The Zenith Starship]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Eurias]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Zenith Devices]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Worldslayer]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Subspace Drive]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Aether]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Aethertech]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Lichborn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[World Spirit of Visiria]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Aethril]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[World Spirit]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Blue (The Last Horizon)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Archmage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Zenith Era]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|water elementalism}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Astral Tide]]}}

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
